back: A digital circuit whose output is solely a function of its present inputs. It has no internal memory or feedback loops.

front: What is the purpose of a multiplexer (MUX)?
back: A combinational circuit that selects one of many input lines and directs it to a single output line. The selection is controlled by a set of select lines.

front: What is a decoder and what is its primary function?
back: A combinational circuit that converts binary information from n input lines to a maximum of 2^n unique output lines. It is often used to select a specific device or memory location.

front: What is the difference between a half-adder and a full-adder?
back: A half-adder adds two single binary digits and produces a sum and a carry output. A full-adder adds three bits (two inputs and a carry-in) and produces a sum and a carry-out.

front: What is meant by a 'don't care' condition in a Karnaugh map?
back: An input combination for which the output of a circuit is unspecified, often because that input can never occur. It can be used as either a 0 or a 1 to help form larger groups and achieve a simpler, more optimal Boolean expression.
